title: antirot build manifest
description: The frozen coordination layer for a course build. Authored in the design phase, approved at the gate, consumed deterministically by build-artifacts.mjs and check.mjs, and passed to the build-course workflow as args. Controls names/order/structure ONLY — it is not the correctness guarantor.

outDir.description: Output directory for the vault, relative to cwd, e.g. ./Confluent-Parallel-Language-Theory

voice.description: Pillar A: the frozen voice contract every lesson-writer must imitate.

exemplar.description: 1-2 paragraph sample passage. Few-shot anchor; writers imitate this, not an adjective list.

notation.description: Pillar A: canonical symbol per recurring object. check.mjs flags divergence.

object.description: Human name of the mathematical/technical object, e.g. 'gradient of f'

firstNote.description: slug of the note that introduces it

id.description: Stable concept id; also the wikilink slug for its home note.

homeNote.description: slug of the note where this concept is canonically defined (the unique `define` site).

criticality.description: critical => routed to the stronger model and to claim-level review. Downstream lessons depend on its correctness.

glossary.description: Appears in the auto-generated Glossary index, which transcludes this concept's canonical definition (^def-<id>) from its home lesson. No separate definition is authored.

groundingRequired.description: If true, the generation workflow runs a researcher to attach real sources before this concept is written/reviewed. Set for niche, cutting-edge, low-confidence, or correctness-critical concepts the model may not reliably know.

sources.description: Real, fetched authoritative references for this concept (populated by the researcher pass, not recalled from memory). Shared by lesson-writer (grounding + citation) and course-reviewer (verification); the Resources appendix is built from these.

note.description: One line on what this source establishes or where to read for depth.

order.description: Global topological index. A `use` link's target must have a strictly lower order.

beats.description: Pillar/pedagogy: the ordered concepts this note covers. A writer may ONLY `define` concepts listed here with kind=define.

linkVocab.description: Closed vocabulary: the ONLY concept ids this note may [[link]] to (its beats + prereqs + glossary). Out-of-vocab links are build failures.

prereqs.description: concept ids the reader must already know

alreadyTaught.description: prereq concept ids the reader has SEEN in an earlier note: link, do not re-teach.

blockIds.description: Declared block ids this note defines, e.g. ['^def-confluence']. Transclusion targets must resolve to one of these.

exercises.description: Planned understanding-checks for this lesson, beyond the per-beat quick checks. Each is rendered as a foldable question + a verified foldable solution. Makes exercises checkable (coverage + solution-present) instead of ad-hoc.

concept.description: primary concept tested; must be in this note's linkVocab

kind.description: cognitive level — prefer apply/derive over recall; recall is the per-beat quick check's job

interleave.description: optional already-taught concept ids this exercise also draws on (spaced retrieval). Must be lower-order concepts.

figures.description: Planned combinatorial-graph figures for this lesson, rendered to SVG by build-figures.mjs from a writer-authored spec. Each slot is checkable: spec present + valid + SVG rendered + embedded. KaTeX/LaTeX cannot draw graphs — use this (or mermaid for simple ones).

id.description: globally-unique figure id; spec at .antirot/figures/<id>.json, renders to assets/<id>.svg

edges.description: Prereq DAG edges. from depends on to (to must be taught first).

to.description: concept id that is the prerequisite

confidence.description: Architect's calibrated confidence. The gate reviews lowest-confidence edges first.

why.description: One-line justification, e.g. 'beta-reduction uses substitution in its definition'.

capstone.description: Optional end-of-module integrative exercise that ties several concepts together. Rendered on the module overview with a foldable solution.

prompt.description: optional seed for the capstone task
